problems.problem_description:
# Problem setup:
Consider a Majorana Sachdev-Ye-Kitaev model with a SIX-body random interaction, $H=\sum_{i_1<\dots<i_6} J_{i_1\dots i_6}\,\chi_{i_1}\cdots\chi_{i_6}$, with Majorana fermions $\{\chi_a,\chi_b\}=\delta_{ab}$ and Gaussian couplings whose variance scales so that the model has a good large-$N$ limit ($\langle J^2\rangle \propto J^2/N^5$). Work in the large-$N$ limit and define the zero-temperature entropy density $S_0/N$ via the low-temperature expansion $\log Z=-\beta E_0+S_0+\tfrac{c}{2\beta}+\cdots$.

# Main problem:
Calculate the numerical value of $S_0/N$ for $q=6$ to four decimal places.
problems.code_template:
import mpmath as mp

def answer():
    r"""Return S0/N for the q=6 Majorana SYK model (float, 4 dp)."""
    # ------------------ FILL IN YOUR RESULTS BELOW ------------------
    S_per_N = ...
    # ---------------------------------------------------------------
    return S_per_N
problems.answer_code:
import mpmath as mp

def answer():
    r"""Return S0/N for the q=6 Majorana SYK model (float, 4 dp)."""
    # ------------------ FILL IN YOUR RESULTS BELOW ------------------
    S_per_N = 0.2908   # reference (verified)
    # ---------------------------------------------------------------
    return S_per_N
